agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Leave management module for HR software governing how employees accrue, request, consume, and carry over paid leave. The module enforces accrual-based balance tracking, advance-notice requirements, manager approval workflows, and year-end carryover caps.","bounded_context":"Leave Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The current number of accrued but unused leave days available to an employee. Cannot be negative."},{"term":"Accrual","definition":"The process by which employees earn 1.5 leave days per month worked, credited to their leave balance."},{"term":"Leave Request","definition":"A formal submission by an employee to take leave for a specified date range, subject to balance availability, advance notice, and manager approval."},{"term":"Medical Leave","definition":"A category of leave that may be submitted retroactively (up to 5 calendar days after the absence) when accompanied by a doctor's note, exempt from the 3-working-day advance notice rule."},{"term":"Working Days","definition":"Business days excluding weekends and public holidays, used to calculate the advance notice period for leave requests."},{"term":"Carryover","definition":"The process at calendar year-end where unused leave balance up to 10 days is retained; any excess is forfeited."},{"term":"Contract End Date","definition":"The date on which an employee's employment contract terminates. Leave requests cannot extend beyond this date."},{"term":"Doctor's Note","definition":"A medical certificate required to validate a retroactive medical leave request."},{"term":"Forfeiture","definition":"The loss of accrued leave days exceeding the 10-day carryover cap at calendar year-end."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Employees accrue 1.5 days of leave per month worked.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Accrual rate is a policy parameter that HR leadership could change. The system must support an accrual rate, but 1.5 days/month is a configurable business policy.","testable_assertion":"After an employee completes N full months of service within the accrual period, their accrued leave increases by exactly N × 1.5 days."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Leave balance cannot go negative. Employees cannot take leave they have not yet accrued.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Negative balance would mean the employee is consuming a resource that does not exist. This is a data consistency constraint — the system state would be corrupt if balance goes below zero.","testable_assertion":"For any approved leave request, the employee's leave balance after deduction must be >= 0."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Non-medical leave requests must be submitted at least 3 working days before the leave start date.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The advance notice period is an operational policy that management could adjust. It is not a physical or data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"A non-medical leave request submitted fewer than 3 working days before the start date is rejected by the system at submission time."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Medical leave can be submitted retroactively with a doctor's note, within 5 days of the absence.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The retroactive window and documentation requirement are HR policy decisions that could be revised.","testable_assertion":"A medical leave request submitted with a valid doctor's note within 5 days of the absence date is accepted; without the note or after 5 days, it is rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Managers can approve or reject leave requests. Only approved leave consumes balance.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If rejected leave consumed balance, the system would be in an inconsistent state — balance deducted for time not actually taken off. This is a state integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"When a manager rejects a leave request, the employee's leave balance remains unchanged from its value before the request was submitted."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Leave requests cannot span across (extend beyond) the employee's contract end date.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"An employee cannot be on approved leave for dates when they are no longer employed. Allowing this would create a logically corrupt state — leave allocated to a non-existent employment relationship.","testable_assertion":"A leave request with any leave day falling on or after the employee's contract end date is rejected at submission time."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"At calendar year-end, unused leave above 10 days is forfeited. Only up to 10 days carry over to the next year.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 10-day carryover cap is a company policy threshold that could be adjusted by HR leadership.","testable_assertion":"On January 1, an employee's carried-over balance equals min(previous_year_end_balance, 10)."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An employee has worked 4 full months with no leave taken","when":"The system calculates their leave balance","then":"The balance is 6.0 days (4 × 1.5)"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 2 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request for 3 days","then":"The request is rejected with an insufficient balance error and balance remains 2 days"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An employee submits a non-medical leave request","when":"The submission date is fewer than 3 working days before the leave start date","then":"The request is rejected with an advance notice violation error"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An employee submits a non-medical leave request","when":"The submission date is exactly 3 working days before the leave start date","then":"The request is accepted for manager review (boundary condition: 3 days is sufficient)"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An employee was absent due to illness on Monday","when":"They submit a medical leave request on the following Friday (within 5 days) with a doctor's note attached","then":"The request is accepted for manager review despite being retroactive"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An employee was absent due to illness on Monday","when":"They submit a medical leave request the following Monday (6 days later) with a doctor's note","then":"The request is rejected as it exceeds the 5-day retroactive submission window"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An employee submits a medical leave request retroactively within 5 days","when":"No doctor's note is attached","then":"The request is rejected due to missing required documentation"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A manager rejects a leave request for 3 days from an employee with a balance of 5 days","when":"The rejection is processed","then":"The employee's leave balance remains 5 days"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"An employee's contract ends on March 15","when":"They submit a leave request for March 13–16","then":"The request is rejected because it spans beyond the contract end date"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An employee has 14 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The year-end carryover process runs","then":"Their balance on January 1 is 10 days and 4 days are recorded as forfeited"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An employee has 8 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The year-end carryover process runs","then":"Their balance on January 1 is 8 days (no forfeiture, under the 10-day cap)"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A leave request has been submitted and is pending manager approval","when":"The employee's leave balance is checked","then":"The pending request's days are not yet deducted from the balance (balance only decreases upon approval)"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Employee submits leave for the exact last day before contract end date","expected_behavior":"Request is accepted for review since it does not extend beyond the contract end date."},{"scenario":"Employee submits non-medical leave on a Friday for the following Wednesday, but Monday is a public holiday","expected_behavior":"The system must count only working days for the 3-day notice period. Friday to Wednesday skipping Monday (holiday) = Fri, Tue, Wed = 2 working days notice, so the request should be rejected."},{"scenario":"Medical leave request submitted on exactly day 5 after the absence","expected_behavior":"Accepted — the 5-day window is inclusive of day 5 (boundary condition)."},{"scenario":"Employee accrues partial month (e.g., starts mid-month or contract ends mid-month)","expected_behavior":"The spec says 'per month worked' — system must define whether partial months accrue proportionally or require a full month. This is an ambiguity in the spec."},{"scenario":"Multiple pending leave requests that collectively exceed balance","expected_behavior":"The system must decide whether pending (unapproved) requests reserve balance or not. If not, two overlapping approvals could overdraw the balance."},{"scenario":"Year-end carryover with pending leave requests","expected_behavior":"System must define whether pending requests are factored into the carryover calculation or only approved/consumed leave counts."},{"scenario":"Employee submits leave request, manager approves, then employee's contract end date is subsequently changed to an earlier date that now conflicts","expected_behavior":"System should validate contract end date at approval time, not just submission time, or retroactively flag the conflict."},{"scenario":"Leave request for a single day that is also the contract end date","expected_behavior":"If contract end date means last day of employment, the employee is still employed that day and the request should be accepted. If it means first day of non-employment, the request should be rejected. The spec is ambiguous on this boundary."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect accrual or carryover logic could result in employees being paid for leave they haven't earned (financial loss) or being denied earned leave (legal liability and potential labor disputes).","operational":"The advance notice requirement ensures managers can plan staffing. Medical leave exceptions ensure compliance with labor law norms around sick leave. Carryover caps prevent liability accumulation on the balance sheet.","user":"Employees need predictable, transparent leave balances. Incorrect rejections erode trust; incorrect approvals create downstream scheduling chaos. Retroactive medical leave is essential for employee wellbeing.","risk":"Primary risks are: (1) balance going negative due to race conditions in concurrent approvals, (2) incorrect working-day calculations leading to wrongful rejections, (3) year-end forfeiture disputes if employees are not notified in advance, (4) legal exposure if medical leave rules are too restrictive relative to local labor law.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Employees","impact":"Directly affected by accrual accuracy, balance visibility, request workflows, and carryover fairness."},{"group":"Managers","impact":"Responsible for approval/rejection decisions; need visibility into team leave schedules and remaining balances."},{"group":"HR/People Operations","impact":"Owns policy configuration, handles disputes, ensures labor law compliance, manages year-end carryover processing."},{"group":"Finance/Payroll","impact":"Leave balances affect payroll calculations, accrued liability reporting, and year-end financial statements."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Medical leave rules and carryover policies must comply with local labor regulations; incorrect implementation creates legal exposure."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is well-articulated with clear rules and no internal contradictions. Confidence is below 80 because several practical edge cases are not addressed: partial-month accrual, the interaction between pending requests and balance/carryover, the precise meaning of 'contract end date' as a boundary, and whether the 5-day medical window counts calendar or working days. These are common ambiguities in leave management that would need HR clarification before implementation.","high_confidence_areas":["Accrual rate of 1.5 days/month","Negative balance prohibition","3 working day advance notice for non-medical leave","Medical leave retroactive exception with doctor's note","Rejected leave does not consume balance","10-day carryover cap with forfeiture"],"low_confidence_areas":["Partial month accrual calculation (pro-rata vs full month only)","Whether 'within 5 days' for medical leave means calendar days or working days","Exact semantics of 'contract end date' — is that the last working day or first non-employment day?","How concurrent/pending leave requests interact with balance checks","Whether balance deduction happens at approval time or leave start date","Notification requirements before year-end forfeiture"],"assumptions":["The 5-day retroactive window for medical leave is measured in calendar days, not working days","Contract end date means the last day of employment (employee is still employed on that date)","Leave balance is deducted upon manager approval, not upon the actual leave dates","The 3 working day notice period excludes the submission day and includes the leave start day","Accrual happens at month-end for completed months"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Leave management module for HR software governing how employees accrue, request, consume, and carry over paid leave. The module enforces accrual-based balance tracking, advance-notice requirements, manager approval workflows, and year-end carryover caps.","bounded_context":"Leave Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The current number of accrued but unused leave days available to an employee. Cannot be negative."},{"term
